task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the Trash folder to locate the deleted papers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Trash' folder is selected/highlighted in the left pane of the Zotero window, displaying the deleted items in the middle pane.

task_completion.description: The agent restores the 5 specified papers from the Trash.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent selects the 5 target papers (Shannon, Turing, Dijkstra, Vaswani, Goodfellow) in the Trash and uses the 'Restore to Library' option (e.g., via right-click context menu or a button).

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the 'Thesis References' collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Thesis References' collection is selected/highlighted in the left pane of the Zotero window.

task_completion.description: The 5 restored papers are successfully added to the 'Thesis References' collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: While the 'Thesis References' collection is selected, the middle pane displays the 5 newly restored papers ('A Mathematical Theory of Communication', 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence', 'A Note on Two Problems in Connexion with Graphs', 'Attention Is All You Need', 'Generative Adversarial Nets').

task_completion.description: The existing items in the 'Thesis References' collection are preserved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: While the 'Thesis References' collection is selected, the middle pane still displays the 3 originally existing papers (BERT/Devlin, Deep Residual Learning/He, ImageNet/Krizhevsky) alongside the newly added papers.

integrity.description: The agent used the Zotero GUI to restore and move items.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks, drag-and-drop actions, or context menus within the Zotero application. The agent does not use a terminal to execute SQL commands on the Zotero database.

integrity.description: The task was performed in the actual Zotero application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard Zotero interface (left pane for collections, middle pane for items list, right pane for metadata) is clearly visible and interacted with.

integrity.description: The agent did not delete or alter the existing items in the collection.
integrity.visual_evidence: The existing items (BERT, ResNet, ImageNet) are never selected for deletion, moved to the Trash, or modified during the trajectory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Collection Name: 'Thesis References'. Trashed papers to restore (5): 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication' (Shannon), 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence' (Turing), 'A Note on Two Problems in Connexion with Graphs' (Dijkstra), 'Attention Is All You Need' (Vaswani), 'Generative Adversarial Nets' (Goodfellow). Existing papers to preserve (3): 'BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding' (Devlin), 'Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition' (He), 'ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks' (Krizhevsky).
